old_claim.note: Standard Time Zones provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim_absent: Employee recorders measure local shift attendance and payroll without requiring continental time-zone coordination.

source_shape.source_locator: 1888 section: dates the Bundy Key Recorder and Dey Dial Recorder to 1888 and explains how they recorded employee identity and shift times for payroll and attendance.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: Bundy and Dey introduced mechanical employee time recorders in 1888 for payroll and attendance.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: The scoped evidence establishes the milestone without the removed dependency. Employee recorders measure local shift attendance and payroll without requiring continental time-zone coordination.

ontology_before: industrial_time_clocks treated standard_time_zones as a direct causal prerequisite.
ontology_after: industrial_time_clocks is scoped to the directly evidenced mechanism and no longer claims standard_time_zones as a prerequisite.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed and locked by graph invariant 2026-07-12-structural-accuracy-batch: industrial_time_clocks no longer directly depends on standard_time_zones.
would_reject_if: A reliable source demonstrates that every implementation within the current industrial_time_clocks scope technically requires standard_time_zones.
short_reason: Employee recorders measure local shift attendance and payroll without requiring continental time-zone coordination.
